meta.educational_goal: To visually explore computational complexity and the boundary between tractable and intractable problems.

states.label: Showing Examples
entry_actions:
alert('Classic NP-Complete problems:

- Boolean satisfiability (SAT)
- Traveling salesman (TSP)
- Graph coloring
- Knapsack problem
- Hamiltonian path
- Subset sum

These all share the fundamental property of NP-Completeness.');
evidence:
document.getElementById('show-examples').addEventListener('click', function() { alert('Classic NP-Complete problems:

- Boolean satisfiability (SAT)
- Traveling salesman (TSP)
- Graph coloring
- Knapsack problem
- Hamiltonian path
- Subset sum

These all share the fundamental property of NP-Completeness.'); });

states.label: Learning More
entry_actions:
alert('To learn more about NP-Completeness:

1. "Computers and Intractability" by Garey & Johnson
2. "Introduction to the Theory of Computation" by Sipser
3. Clay Mathematics Institute's Millennium Problems

This is one of the deepest questions in theoretical computer science!');
evidence:

This is one of the deepest questions in theoretical computer science!'); });
